name: Glazed Pork Chops w Pineapple, Watermelon & Ginger Relish
recipeIngredient: 4 pork loin chops (trimmed to leave about 1/4-inch fat)
1 cup diced fresh or canned pineapple
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the pork chops with salt, pepper, and cayenne.
2. Heat olive oil in a skillet over moderately high heat until almost smoking.
3. Cook the pork chops for about 2 to 3 minutes per side, or until well browned on both sides. Remove chops to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
4. Deglaze the skillet with 1 cup pineapple juice, stirring to remove browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
5. Stir in 1 tablespoon minced gingerroot and 2 tablespoons brown sugar. Bring the sauce to a simmer.
6. Return the pork chops and any collected juices to the skillet. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 6 to 8 minutes until the meat is cooked through. Remove chops to a plate and cover with foil to keep warm.
7. If necessary, simmer the sauce until slightly reduced. Mix together cornstarch and cold water in a separate bowl (1 teaspoon each), then stir into the simmering sauce. Continue stirring until thickened. Reduce heat, add mustard, and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
8. In a bowl combine diced pineapple, watermelon, remaining gingerroot, scallions, jalapeno, parsley, lime juice, and 1/3 cup pineapple juice. Mix well.
9. Serve the pork chops warm, glazed with the sauce, and garnished with the pineapple & watermelon relish.
